description_long: This object consists of a set of two study carrels with attached chairs and a separate open shelving unit. The carrels feature high privacy panels on three sides, providing a secluded workspace for each user, and are paired with minimalist chairs that have a contoured seat and backrest. The shelving unit has three open shelves, suitable for storing books, supplies, or personal items, and matches the carrels in color and material. The surfaces of both the carrels and shelving unit are flat and smooth, likely made from laminated wood or engineered wood, with a matte finish. The overall design is utilitarian and modern, suitable for public spaces such as libraries, study rooms, or community centers. The color scheme is a uniform dark gray, giving the set a cohesive and understated appearance.
description: A modern set of two study carrels with privacy panels and attached chairs, paired with a matching open shelving unit in dark gray. The design is utilitarian and suitable for public or urban environments.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open shelving unit on the left and the two carrels with attached chairs and privacy panels in the center and right.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the carrels, the privacy panels, and the arrangement of the chairs behind the desks.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back panels of the carrels and the open shelving unit, with the chairs partially visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the side profiles of the carrels and shelving unit, emphasizing the thickness of the panels and the arrangement of the chairs.
